Name: Aaron Gregory
College: College of Arts, Humanities & Social Sciences
Department: Native American Studies Department, Environmental Resources Engineering
Title: Assistant Professor
Research_Interests: Research Interests: Explores lithium as a source of energetic and political power, And as a more-than-human actor that enrolls and articulates a range of state, Technoscientific and indigenous actors into new socio-technical kinship relations. other areas of interest: science & technology studies (sts), Critical infrastructure studies, Indigenous studies, Political ecology, Land restitution and restoration, Ecological personhood, Postcolonial theories, Decolonizing research methodologies, And community-based research.
Teaching_Interests: Teaching Interests: Teaches courses in native american studies, Focusing on the intersection of indigenous knowledge systems and contemporary environmental issues. involved in organizing academic events, Such as the 4th annual post-capitalism conference and decolonizing economies summit, Where he co-organized a panel on food & seed sovereignty highlighting the rou dalagurr food sovereignty lab and traditional ecological knowledge institute at cal poly humboldt.
Research_Interests_List: Research Interests: Explores lithium as a source of energetic and political power, And as a more-than-human actor that enrolls and articulates a range of state, Technoscientific and indigenous actors into new socio-technical kinship relations. other areas of interest: science & technology studies (sts), Critical infrastructure studies, Indigenous studies, Political ecology, Land restitution and restoration, Ecological personhood, Postcolonial theories, Decolonizing research methodologies, And community-based research.
